channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pete Hegseth calls himself 'change agent' as Senate hearing opens for Trump's defense secretary pick
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate opened a potentially explosive confirmation hearing Tuesday for President-elect Donald Trump’s choice for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, who calls himself a “change agent” as senators determine whether the former combat veteran and TV news show host is fit to lead the
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, TARA COPP and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken will make a case for post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is set to make a last-minute case for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden DHS exempted thousands of immigrants from terror-related entry restrictions in FY 2024
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security gave more than 6,800 exemptions to terrorism-related entry bars, a significantly higher number that in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Planned Parenthood chapter provided Harris campaign workspace, breaking tax law: IRS complaint
item.description: A Planned Parenthood chapter is accused of violating its tax-exempt status by hosting Harris-Walz 2024 campaign offices, per an IRS complaint by 40 Days for Life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Freedom Caucus takes over the Wyoming House, marking its first chance to lead
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — As President-elect Donald Trump plans bold moves for his first days in office, so too are conservative lawmakers in Wyoming, the first state where Trump-friendly Freedom Caucus members have won control of a statehouse chamber.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An American tradition: Defeated candidates attending the president-elect's inauguration
item.description: In January 1981, Jimmy Carter nodded politely toward Ronald Reagan as the new Republican president thanked the Democrat for his administration's help after Reagan resoundingly defeated Carter the previous November.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to watch as Pete Hegseth faces questions about his experience and personal past
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth will publicly face senators for the first time Tuesday after weeks of privately pushing back on criticism over his qualifications and personal past.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley bill past second hurdle
item.description: The Laken Riley Act on Monday cleared a second procedural hurdle in the Senate as talk turns to an amendment push by Democrats in order to potentially make alterations to the bill. Senators voted 82-10 on the motion to proceed to the legislation, allowing the chamber to begin debate.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carrie Underwood, Village People to perform at Trump's inauguration events
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Country music star Carrie Underwood will perform “America the Beautiful” at Donald Trump's inauguration next week and the 1970s hitmakers Village People will perform at two inaugural events.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Special Counsel Weiss blasts Biden in final Hunter prosecution report
item.description: The Justice Department released Special Counsel David Weiss' report on his years-long investigation into Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state county ignores ICE detainers against illegal immigrant charged with rape
item.description: Boston ICE agents had to arrest a Brazilian illegal immigrant charged with rape and extortion twice because of sanctuary policies in the state of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats eye effort to secure Jack Smith's Trump Jan. 6 report
item.description: The top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee is asking the Justice Department to retain all records relating to special counsel Jack Smith, including his final report which has been tied up in litigation. Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California lawmakers postpone special session to 'Trump-proof' state due to wildfires
item.description: California lawmakers postponed a special session on legislation meant to "Trump-proof" the state ahead of the president-elect's inauguration, due to wildfires around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's when Trump nominees will appear for confirmation hearings
item.description: More than a dozen people nominated by President-elect Trump to serve in his administration will make their case to Senate committees this week. The busy week will kick off with the Senate Armed Services Committee hearing from Pete Hegseth on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump planning to expand Be Best campaign in return as first lady
item.description: Melania Trump says she's aiming to expand her children's wellness platform, Be Best, when she returns to the White House as first lady. "I will continue with Be Best and also I will expand Be Best," Trump said in a Monday interview on "Fox & Friends.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California's working class overlooked amid wildfires, US rep says
item.description: Rep. Maxine Waters, D-Calif., called for attention to the working class people in Altadena, California, who have “limited resources" amid the wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5
